prompt: What device tells you which direction is north using a magnetic needle?

What two-wheeled, pedal-powered vehicle is steered with handlebars and ridden for transportation or sport?
What small electronic device with a keypad performs arithmetic and other mathematical calculations?
What large household appliance uses a refrigerant cycle to keep food and drinks cold?

What flat, polished surface reflects light to show a clear image of whatever stands before it?
What office tool drives small metal fasteners through sheets of paper to bind them together?
What tool with a weighted metal head on a handle is used to drive nails into wood?
What long, straight-edged strip marked with unit measurements is used to measure length and draw straight lines?
What folded paper container with a sealable flap is used to send letters through the mail?
What long-handled cleaning tool with bristles at one end is used to sweep dust and debris from floors?
What cylindrical wax object with a central wick is burned to provide light?
What small metal object with a unique pattern of notches is inserted into a lock to open it?
What eating utensil has a shallow oval bowl on a handle and is used to scoop food or liquids?
What eating utensil with multiple pointed tines is used to spear and lift solid food?
What small open container with a handle is used to hold and drink hot or cold beverages?
What round, deep dish is used to hold soup, cereal, or other food at a meal?
What flat, round dish is used to hold and serve solid food at a meal?
What writing instrument uses liquid ink stored inside to leave marks on paper?
What slender writing instrument uses a graphite core to leave marks on paper that can be erased?
What small block of rubber is used to remove pencil marks from paper?
What bag with two shoulder straps worn on the back is used to carry books and belongings?
What small timepiece is strapped to the wrist for convenient timekeeping?
What pair of corrective lenses in a frame is worn on the face to improve vision?
What soft cushion filled with feathers or foam is placed under the head during sleep?
What large piece of soft fabric is draped over the body to provide warmth in bed?
What thick, absorbent cloth is used to dry the body after washing or swimming?
What cleansing product in bar or liquid form is used with water to wash away dirt and germs?
What small brush with a long handle and soft bristles is used to clean the teeth?
What flat grooming tool with a row of thin teeth is used to separate and smooth hair?
What long strip of leather or fabric worn around the waist keeps trousers from falling down?
What fastening device uses two strips of interlocking metal or plastic teeth, opened and closed by a sliding tab?
What thin, pointed metal instrument with an eye for thread is used to stitch fabric together by hand?
What narrow strip coated with adhesive on one side is used to fasten or seal lightweight materials?
What sticky liquid or paste is applied to surfaces to bond them together as it dries?
What thin rubber bag inflated with air or helium is used as a decoration at parties?
What device uses a lens to focus light onto a sensor or film to record photographs?
What portable electrochemical cell stores electrical energy and releases it to power devices?
What small handheld wireless device sends infrared signals to operate a television from across the room?
What portable structure with two rails joined by evenly spaced rungs is used to climb up or down?
What open-topped cylindrical container with a handle is used to carry water and other materials?
What strong, flexible length of twisted fiber is used for tying, hauling, or climbing?
What circular disc that rotates on a central axle is the fundamental mechanism enabling wheeled vehicles to move?
What flat printed or drawn representation of a geographic area shows roads, rivers, and landmarks?
What spherical model mounted on a stand has the continents, oceans, and countries of the Earth printed on its surface?
What small, flat metal disc issued by a government is used as a form of currency?
What small adhesive label affixed to an envelope or parcel shows that postage for delivery has been paid?
What chart organized by days, weeks, and months is used to track the passage of time through a year?
What device with a platform or digital display is used to measure the weight of objects placed on it?
What battery-powered handheld device emits a concentrated beam of light from an electric bulb?
What tool with a flat or pointed metal blade fixed to a long wooden handle is used for digging earth?
What tool with a serrated metal blade is moved back and forth to cut through wood or other materials?
What tool with soft bristles fixed to a handle is used to apply paint or varnish to surfaces?
What object produces an invisible field that attracts iron and steel and can hold notes to a metal surface?
What portable enclosed light source protects a flame or electric bulb from wind and weather?
What small, colored wax stick is used by children to draw and color on paper?
What bound collection of printed pages containing text or illustrations is published for reading?
What large, regularly printed publication on thin paper contains news articles and current events?
What household electronic device receives broadcast signals and displays moving images and audio on a screen?
What bedside device is set in advance to produce a loud sound at a specific time in order to wake a person?
What small hollow instrument produces a shrill, loud sound when a person blows air through it?
What ancient counting frame has beads that slide on rods and was used for arithmetic before calculators existed?
What navigational instrument measures the angle between a celestial body and the horizon to determine a ship's position at sea?
What instrument measures atmospheric pressure and is used to help predict changes in the weather?
What optical instrument using mirrors or prisms allows a submarine crew to observe the sea surface without surfacing?
What ancient timekeeping device uses the shadow of an upright arm cast by sunlight to indicate the hour of day?
What early electrical device transmits coded messages as pulses of current along a wire, widely used in the 19th century?
What early sound-reproduction machine invented by Thomas Edison plays recordings etched onto a rotating grooved cylinder or disc?
What mechanical device with a keyboard prints letters one at a time onto paper by striking inked type against it?
What powered machine uses a needle and interlocking threads to stitch fabric together far faster than hand sewing?
What optical instrument uses multiple lenses to magnify objects too small to be seen clearly with the naked eye?
What optical instrument uses lenses or mirrors to make distant objects such as stars appear closer and larger?
What medical instrument worn around a doctor's neck transmits the sounds of the heart and lungs to earpieces?
What mechanical or electronic device produces a steady audible tick at an adjustable rate to help musicians keep time?
What two-pronged metal implement vibrates at a precise, fixed pitch when struck, used to tune musical instruments?
What laboratory gas burner produces a single, adjustable open flame used to heat equipment during experiments?
What laboratory machine spins samples at high speed in order to separate their components according to density?
What heavy block of iron or steel provides a hard surface on which a blacksmith hammers hot metal to shape it?
What machine tool holds and rotates a workpiece while a cutting tool shapes it into a cylindrical form?
What power tool uses a rotating bit to bore holes into wood, metal, or masonry?
What hand tool with shaped jaws grips and turns nuts, bolts, and pipe fittings?
What hinged hand tool with serrated jaws grips, bends, or cuts wire and small components?
What tool contains a bubble trapped in a liquid-filled tube to indicate when a surface is perfectly horizontal or vertical?
What tool with a hardened metal blade and a handle is driven by a mallet to carve or cut wood, stone, or metal?
What electrically heated tool melts soft metal solder to form permanent joints between electronic components?
What electronic test instrument measures voltage, electric current, and resistance in a single device?
What computer component stores digital data on spinning magnetic platters enclosed in a sealed metal housing?
What device modulates and demodulates signals to connect a computer to the internet via telephone or cable lines?
What device uses a moving beam of light to convert printed documents and photographs into digital image files?
What device uses a bright light source and lens to enlarge and display images or video onto a large screen?
What device senses room temperature and automatically activates a heating or cooling system to maintain a set level?
What medical device delivers a controlled electric shock to the chest to restore a normal heart rhythm during cardiac arrest?
What medical device directs electromagnetic radiation through the body to produce images of internal structures such as bones?
What scientific instrument detects and records vibrations in the ground caused by earthquakes or other seismic events?
What handheld instrument detects and measures ionizing radiation such as alpha particles, beta particles, and gamma rays?
What detection system emits sound waves underwater and listens for their echoes to locate submarines and map the sea floor?
What detection system sends out radio waves and analyzes their reflections to track the position and speed of aircraft and ships?
What handheld or vehicle-mounted device calculates its exact position on Earth by receiving timing signals from orbiting satellites?
What office device scans a document and transmits its image over a telephone line to be printed by a matching machine elsewhere?
What rectangular plastic housing containing a spool of magnetic tape was widely used to record and play audio from the 1970s through the 1990s?
What thin, flexible magnetic storage disc enclosed in a square plastic case was an early removable medium for personal computers?
What shiny circular optical disc stores digital data, including music and software, that is read by a laser?
What small plug-in device uses flash memory chips to store and transfer digital files between computers?
What ceiling-mounted safety device sounds a loud alarm when it detects smoke particles in the air?
What pressurized cylindrical canister is aimed at the base of a fire and discharged to release a smothering agent?
What spinning wheel or disc mounted in a set of rings maintains its orientation in space regardless of how the surrounding frame is tilted?
What instrument measures the altitude of an aircraft above sea level, typically by detecting changes in air pressure?
What instrument measures wind speed, typically using a set of small rotating cups that spin faster as the wind blows harder?
What instrument measures the amount of water vapor, or humidity, present in the air?
What handheld instrument measures the angle of inclination or slope of a hillside, roof, or other surface?
What mechanical linkage instrument traces an original drawing with a stylus and simultaneously copies it at a different scale?
What electronic instrument displays the waveform of electrical signals as a moving line graph on a screen over time?
What handheld device used by police measures the concentration of alcohol in a person's exhaled breath?
What instrument records physiological responses such as blood pressure and breathing rate to assess whether a person may be lying?
What cassette format for magnetic video tape dominated the home video market during the 1980s and 1990s?
What kitchen appliance uses electromagnetic radiation in the microwave frequency to heat food rapidly from the inside out?
What household machine sprays hot water and detergent over dishes and utensils to clean them automatically?
What household appliance uses water, a rotating drum, and detergent to launder clothing and linens?
What small kitchen appliance uses electric heating elements to brown slices of bread by applying dry radiant heat?
What kitchen appliance uses rapidly rotating blades in a tall container to puree, chop, or mix food and liquids?
What sealed cooking pot traps steam to raise internal pressure, allowing food to cook at temperatures above the boiling point of water?
What instrument is connected in parallel across a component to measure the electrical potential difference, or voltage, across it?
What small belt-worn wireless device beeps or vibrates when it receives a short numeric or text message from a remote system?
What manual calculating device uses two logarithmic scales that slide past each other to perform multiplication, division, and other calculations?
What precision measuring instrument uses a main scale and a sliding vernier scale to measure dimensions such as diameter and depth to fractions of a millimeter?
What heavy weight suspended on a string uses gravity to establish a perfectly vertical reference line?
What semicircular or circular measuring tool marked in degrees is used to measure and lay out angles on paper or a workpiece?
What drawing instrument with two hinged legs, one holding a sharp point and the other a pencil or pen, is used to draw circles and arcs?
What highly precise timekeeping instrument, developed in the 18th century, allowed ships to determine their longitude by keeping exact time at sea?
What surveying instrument mounts a precision telescope on horizontal and vertical axes to measure angles in both planes with great accuracy?
What precision measuring instrument uses a calibrated screw mechanism to measure very small dimensions such as wire diameter to within micrometers?
What instrument measures the pressure of a gas or liquid, often using a U-shaped tube partially filled with fluid?
What instrument measures extremely high temperatures from a safe distance by analyzing the thermal radiation emitted by a hot object?
What pressurized chamber uses superheated steam at high pressure to sterilize surgical instruments and laboratory equipment?
What simple instrument detects the presence and sign of electric charge by showing the deflection of thin metal leaves inside a glass vessel?
What instrument measures the strength, direction, and relative change of a magnetic field in its surrounding environment?
What highly sensitive instrument measures tiny local variations in gravitational acceleration at the Earth's surface?
What instrument measures the rotational speed of an engine shaft or other rotating device, typically expressed in revolutions per minute?
What instrument measures the force, torque, or mechanical power output of an engine or motor?
What instrument measures the viscosity of a liquid, which is its resistance to flow under an applied force?
What insulated vessel measures the heat released or absorbed during a chemical reaction or physical change?
What optical instrument disperses light or other radiation into its component wavelengths to identify the chemical composition of a sample?
What precision instrument uses the interference of overlapping light waves to make extremely accurate measurements of distance, wavelength, or refractive index?
What highly sensitive instrument measures the power of incident electromagnetic radiation by detecting the heating effect on an absorbing element?
What 19th-century weaving machine used a chain of punched cards to automatically control thread patterns, making it an early example of programmable technology?
What clockwork mechanical model, powered by gears in a cabinet, demonstrates the relative positions and orbital motions of the planets around the sun?
What mechanical instrument measures the area of an irregular two-dimensional shape by tracing its boundary with a moving stylus?
What hand-cranked electrostatic generator, developed in the 1880s, uses two counter-rotating discs to accumulate very high voltage static electricity?
What early electrical capacitor, consisting of a glass jar coated inside and outside with metal foil, was used in 18th-century experiments to store static electric charge?
What sensitive electromagnetic instrument detects and measures very small electric currents by the deflection of a magnetic needle or moving coil?
What ancient Greek bronze device, recovered from a 1st-century BCE shipwreck, used interlocking gears to predict eclipses and astronomical positions?
What compact type of barometer uses a sealed flexible metal capsule that expands or contracts with air pressure, eliminating the need for liquid mercury?
What geophysical instrument detects and measures very slight changes in the tilt angle of the ground surface, used to monitor volcanic activity and fault zones?
What mechanical or electronic device fitted in a vehicle records the total distance that vehicle has traveled since it was manufactured?
What astronomical instrument uses a motor-driven flat mirror to redirect sunlight into a fixed solar telescope while compensating for Earth's rotation throughout the day?
What extremely sensitive analytical balance detects mass changes as small as one microgram, used in thin-film deposition and adsorption research?
What instrument measures the coefficient of friction between two surfaces under controlled conditions of load and sliding speed?
What instrument measures the change in length or volume of a material as its temperature is varied, used to study thermal expansion?
What optical instrument measures the refractive index of a transparent substance, used to determine its concentration or chemical identity?
What optical instrument uses polarized light to detect internal stresses in glass or crystals and to analyze birefringent materials?
What wood-splitting tool has a blade oriented perpendicular to its handle and is struck with a mallet to cleave wood along the grain?
What woodworking tool has a curved or flat blade set at right angles to its handle and is swung like an axe to chip and smooth timber surfaces?
What woodworking tool has a long blade with a handle at each end and is pulled toward the user to pare and shape wood?
What small planing tool with a blade flanked by two short handles is used to smooth and shape curved wooden pieces such as chair legs and wheel spokes?
What cylindrical rod or shaft is inserted into a workpiece as a core around which metal or other material is shaped, bent, or wound?
What rotary cutting tool with fluted edges is used to enlarge a previously drilled hole to an accurate diameter with a smooth finish?
What multi-toothed linear cutting tool is pushed or pulled through a hole to cut a specific internal profile such as a keyway or spline?
What hardened steel tool with fluted cutting edges is turned into a pre-drilled hole to cut internal screw threads?
What metalworking tool or die reduces the cross-section of a rod or wire, or forms a specific contoured shape on the surface of a workpiece?
What circular surgical saw, shaped like a hollow cylinder with serrated edges, is used to cut a disc of bone from the skull?
What sharp-pointed surgical instrument enclosed in a hollow cannula is used to puncture a body cavity in order to drain fluid or introduce a laparoscope?
What surgical instrument holds the cut edges of an incision open to give the surgeon unobstructed access to deeper tissues?
What handheld instrument projects a focused beam of light into the eye so a physician can examine the retina, optic disc, and blood vessels at the back of the eye?
What medical instrument with a curved blade and a built-in light source is inserted into the mouth to visualize the larynx and vocal cords, often during intubation?
What set of reference minerals of known hardness is used to determine the hardness of an unknown mineral by scratch testing?
What optical instrument measures changes in the polarization state of light reflected from a thin film surface to determine the film's thickness and optical constants?
What analytical instrument detects minuscule changes in mass deposited on a quartz crystal by measuring the resulting shift in the crystal's resonant frequency?
What large, double-sided coarse file with cutting teeth is used by a farrier to level and shape a horse's hoof wall before fitting a shoe?
What sheet-metal cutting tool punches out small overlapping notches along a line to cut curves and complex shapes without distorting the surrounding metal?
What vacuum tube generates and focuses a beam of electrons onto a phosphorescent screen to display text and images, used in older televisions and computer monitors?
What stiff paper card with holes punched in specific positions was used to store data and program instructions for early computers and automated looms?
What device consisting of a flexible expanding chamber with a nozzle blows a directed stream of air when compressed, used to stoke a fire in a forge or fireplace?
What tapered wooden rod weighted at one end is used to twist raw fiber into yarn during hand spinning?
What ancient granodiorite stele inscribed with the same royal decree in hieroglyphics, Demotic script, and ancient Greek enabled scholars to decipher Egyptian hieroglyphics?
What legendary sword, wielded by King Arthur in British mythology, was given to him by the Lady of the Lake?
What famous cracked bell, housed in Philadelphia, was historically rung to mark important events in early American history including the first public reading of the Declaration of Independence?
What series of ornate jeweled Easter eggs, crafted from precious metals and gemstones, was made by the House of Faberge as imperial gifts for the Russian tsars?
What large, deep-blue natural diamond famous for its supposed curse is now on permanent display at the Smithsonian National Museum of Natural History?
What ancient water-lifting device attributed to Archimedes uses a rotating helical surface inside a cylinder to raise water from a lower level to a higher one?

What frame-based device interlaces two perpendicular sets of threads to produce woven cloth?
What staff or forked stick holds a bundle of unspun fiber such as flax or wool steady while a spinner draws it out and twists it into thread?
What laboratory instrument measures how much light of each wavelength a substance absorbs or transmits, used to identify and quantify dissolved materials?
What instrument measures the deformation and flow behavior of materials, particularly complex fluids and soft solids, under applied stress or strain?
